title: From Russia to Middle East: How India is managing to secure its oil supplies amid Iran war
content: According to the government, India has around 60 days of petroleum supplies in various forms including strategic reserves. But if 20% of the world’s crude supply remains disrupted over 2.5 months of the war, where is India getting its oil from?

title: Bill Gates Foundation reduces its Microsoft stake to zero from 28.5m
content: Meta is laying off around 8,000 employees on Wednesday, May 20, with notification emails going out in three regional waves starting at 4 AM local time. A leaked memo from HR chief Janelle Gale tells North American staff to work from home as the cuts roll out. Meanwhile, 7,000 workers are being reassigned to four new AI-focused organisations as Mark Zuckerberg restructures Meta around flatter teams, fewer managers, and a $145 billion AI bet.

title: Sooryavanshi scripts history, becomes first-ever Indian player to...
content: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi created IPL history by becoming the first Indian batter to hit 50 sixes in a single season. The 15-year-old smashed 93 off 38 balls, including 10 sixes, for Rajasthan Royals against Lucknow Super Giants. His explosive innings helped Rajasthan stay in the chase after Mitchell Marsh’s 96 powered LSG to 220/5.

title: Marco Rubio set for India visit amid upcoming Quad talks in New Delhi
content: India is set to host a crucial Quadrilateral Security Dialogue (Quad) meeting, with US Secretary of State Marco Rubio expected to attend. This high-level gathering, involving India, the US, Japan, and Australia, is seen as a strategic move to counter China's influence in the Indo-Pacific. The meeting will likely address critical mineral supply chains and regional security concerns.

title: Anthropic CEO makes a 'doomsday' prediction for software companies
content: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei told WSJ at the World Economic Forum in Davos that software is heading toward being "cheap, maybe essentially free," warning that whole careers built around writing code may not survive the AI shift. The Anthropic chief executive also predicted SaaS incumbents leaning on code complexity as a moat could "go bust" as ServiceNow, Snowflake and Microsoft stocks slide and Anthropic eyes a $900 billion valuation.

title: Watch: DRDO’s UAV-fired missile destroys airborne target during air & ground combat trials
content: India's defence sector achieved a significant milestone with the successful final trials of the Unmanned Aerial Vehicle Launched Precision Guided Missile (ULPGM)-V3.  Developed entirely domestically, the missile demonstrated potent anti-tank and air-to-air capabilities, bolstering India's self-reliance in defence production and marking a strategic advancement for the nation's indigenous defence ecosystem.

title: Bill Gates Foundation reduces its Microsoft stake to Zero from 28.5 million, a move driven by…
content: The Bill Gates Foundation Trust sold its entire Microsoft (MSFT) stake in Q1 2026, offloading the final 7.7 million shares worth $3.2 billion. The exit caps a two-year wind-down from 28.5 million shares, funding Gates' plan to spend the endowment by 2045. Microsoft stock slipped to $422.07 even as Pershing Square's Bill Ackman disclosed a fresh $2.3 billion MSFT buy the same day.

title: 'Was an hour away': Trump on halting planned military attack on Iran
content: US President Donald Trump revealed the United States was hours from striking Iran, with warships ready, before a diplomatic opening prompted a pause. He warned of further "big hits" if negotiations fail, but Gulf leaders urged him to hold off. Iran's army countered by threatening to "open new fronts" if strikes resume.

title: 'Yeah… Mohsin': Pant and Ian Bishop’s hilarious toss chat on Shami goes viral - Watch
content: RR made three changes with Sandeep Sharma, Sushant Mishra and Lhuan-dre Pretorious coming into the playing XI.

title: CEA says India facing ‘live balance of payments stress test’: What it means
content: The CEA’s words could not be more direct and the situation the world’s sixth largest economy faces is by no means small. Several macroeconomic indicators are in focus; inflation, health of the current account, and the exchange rate.

title: Is AI gunning for your job? The truth is far from white & blue
content: AI adoption is reshaping the job market, with significant tech job cuts reported and AI capable of performing a substantial portion of the US labor market's work. While AI automates tasks rather than eliminating entire jobs, younger workers in entry-level roles are disproportionately affected.

title: Watch: Rizwan antics rile up Litton; heated exchange follows in 2nd Test
content: Tensions rose during the second Test between Bangladesh national cricket team and Pakistan national cricket team as Mohammad Rizwan and Litton Das exchanged heated words over movement near the sight screen. Umpire Allahudien Paleker intervened to calm the situation. Meanwhile, Bangladesh tightened their grip on the match, leaving Pakistan at 316/7 chasing 437, with Rizwan unbeaten on 75.

title: How Vijay’s astrologer row put spotlight on politicians’ ‘star’ connection
content: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay appointed his spiritual advisor as Officer on Special Duty. Public outcry led to the swift revocation of this appointment. This event underscores the deep-rooted influence of astrology in Indian politics. Many leaders privately consult astrologers for crucial decisions. This practice continues despite public scrutiny and modern governance.

title: TVK alliance in trouble? CPM threatens to pull support to Vijay govt over AIADMK rebels
content: The CPM has warned it will reconsider its support for the Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam government. This comes if the ruling party includes the rebel faction of the AIADMK in its cabinet. CPM state secretary P Shanmugam stated this at a press conference. The party's support was initially to prevent President's Rule and BJP's influence.

title: Months after snubbing Trump, Nato weighs Hormuz mission to protect ships
content: NATO is considering deploying forces to protect shipping in the Strait of Hormuz if the blockade persists into July, a move that would significantly alter the alliance's stance on the US-Israeli conflict with Iran. Growing economic pressure from soaring energy prices is driving this reconsideration, with leaders set to discuss the issue at an upcoming summit.

title: Fashion giant 'Mango' founder Isak Andic’s son arrested over tycoon’s death in 2024
content: Spanish police arrested Jonathan Andic, son of late Mango founder Isak Andic, in connection with his father's December 2024 death. Initially ruled an accident, the case reopened after inconsistencies in Jonathan's testimony emerged. Family sources express full confidence in his innocence, awaiting further investigation.

title: Garlands, roadshow: Rape accused gets rousing welcome in UP after bail - video
content: An alleged rape accused in Ghaziabad received a triumphant welcome from supporters upon his bail, with videos showing him being carried on shoulders amidst cheers and a procession. Samajwadi Party chief Akhilesh Yadav criticized the state government, calling the reception reprehensible and highlighting the perceived hypocrisy.

title: Who is Gurnoor Brar? The Dale Steyn fan picked for Afghanistan Test, ODI series
content: Spotted early by Shubman Gill, backed by Shikhar Dhawan and mentored by Ashish Nehra, Punjab pacer Gurnoor Brar’s rise has been shaped by pace, setbacks and learning curves. From a late start to India A success, the six-foot-five quick is now firmly on the national radar as India search for fast-bowling depth. The lanky fast bowler on Tuesday has received a maiden Indian call -up for both Tests and ODIs.

title: Promise or pressure? Why TMC candidate Jahangir Khan withdrew from Falta repoll
content: Trinamool Congress candidate Jahangir Khan withdrew from Falta's re-poll, citing a "special package" for the area's development. The TMC, however, stated his decision was personal, alleging pressure and intimidation against their workers by the BJP. The Election Commission ordered re-polling due to "severe electoral offences" and subversion of the democratic process.

title: Indian protocol official found dead at Assistant High Commission in Bangladesh
content: An assistant protocol officer at the Indian High Commission in Chattogram, Narendra, 38, was found dead inside the mission premises on Tuesday. Police suspect he may have suffered a cardiac arrest while going to the bathroom during the night. The body has been sent for an autopsy to determine the exact cause of death.

title: NTA revamp after NEET leak: Pradhan orders foolproof re-test
content: Amid the NEET-UG 2026 paper leak controversy, the NTA has announced significant institutional and technological reforms, including hiring a CTO and strengthening oversight. Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan chaired a review meeting, emphasizing secure and foolproof conduct for the upcoming re-examination, with enhanced coordination with state authorities.

title: Meet the world's richest cat who is worth $200m dollars
content: But even the lower reported number is staggering. If one uses the $1.5 million figure and compares it with the median net worth of an American family, about $192,900 in the most recent Federal Reserve survey, Choupette’s supposed care arrangement works out to roughly 7.8 times that figure. Round it off, and the headline becomes irresistible: this cat may be worth eight times the typical American family. If one uses the higher $4 million rumour, the number rises to about 20.7 times the median American family net worth.

title: 'CM Adhikari promised ...': Jahangir Khan exits Falta repoll; TMC says 'succumbed to pressure'
content: Trinamool Congress candidate Jahangir Khan has withdrawn his nomination for the Falta re-poll, days before voting. The Election Commission had previously scrapped the original polls due to severe electoral offenses and subversion of the democratic process. Observer reports highlighted significant irregularities, including EVM tampering and voter intimidation. This development follows criticism from West Bengal Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari.

title: Dia Mirza had no vanity van during ‘RHTDM’, recalls Daisy Shah
content: Recalling early 2000s shoots, Daisy Shah and Dia Mirza highlight the stark absence of vanity vans and basic facilities.  Actresses once changed in malls or restaurants, and faced freezing temperatures during outdoor filming.  Despite hardships, production teams eventually provided support, showcasing a significant shift in industry amenities.

title: Bumrah, Jadeja rested for AFG series as Prince Yadav earns maiden call-up
content: India have announced squads for the Afghanistan one-off Test and ODI series, with Ravindra Jadeja rested from the series. Rohit Sharma and Virat Kohli feature in the ODI squad, though Rohit and Hardik Pandya’s participation remains subject to fitness clearance. Shubman Gill will lead India in both formats, while Gurnoor Brar, Harsh Dubey and Prince Yadav are among the notable inclusions.

title: Language-based AI systems could aid in organising archaeology reports
content: A new book explores Indian archaeology's post-independence journey, highlighting the decolonization challenges faced by the ASI. It emphasizes Amalananda Ghosh's impactful leadership and discusses the ongoing issues of training and employment for archaeologists. The article also touches upon the potential of AI in aiding archaeological research and data management.

title: Builder hasn't delivered your flat? What RERA says and how to file a complaint
content: Homebuyers facing delayed possession or unfair practices by builders now have legal recourse under RERA. The Act empowers buyers to seek compensation for delays or opt for a full refund with interest.  It's crucial to file complaints early with supporting documents to protect your rights and investment.

title: Before Tejas, there was Marut: India’s first supersonic fighter jet & lessons it offers
content: India's ambitious Marut supersonic fighter, designed by Kurt Tank, faced early setbacks due to underfunded engines, limiting its performance. Despite this, it proved effective in the 1971 war, destroying enemy tanks. Bureaucratic indecision ultimately doomed upgrades, mirroring current challenges with the Tejas program's engine development and integration, highlighting a recurring theme in India's aviation history.

title: Punjabi singer Yashinder Kaur found dead days after being kidnapped at gunpoint in Ludhiana
content: A Punjabi singer, Inder Kaur, was found dead in Neelon Canal five days after her alleged kidnapping at gunpoint. Her car, found nearby with blood stains, suggests she was murdered before being dumped. Police have arrested the father and friend of her former boyfriend, Sukhwinder Singh, who recently returned from Canada.

title: India, UAE forge deep defence alliance: $5bn investment, joint production of drones & missiles
content: India and the UAE have significantly deepened their defence ties in 2026, moving towards joint industrial production of advanced weaponry and enhanced maritime and cyber security. This strategic partnership, backed by substantial UAE investment, aims to bolster India's defence manufacturing and secure vital trade routes amidst regional instability.

title: What RR, PBKS, KKR, DC and CSK need to do to grab the final IPL 2026 playoff spot
content: The race for the final IPL playoff spot intensifies with five teams vying for one berth. Rajasthan Royals are best positioned, needing just one win, while Punjab Kings control their destiny. Kolkata Knight Riders, Delhi Capitals, and Chennai Super Kings require victories and favorable results elsewhere. Every remaining match significantly impacts the playoff picture.

title: Doctor Couple, 11-Year-Old Harassed By Cops At Gujarat Temple, Action Follows
content: The doctor couple were humiliated over a false criminal complaint and their 11-year-old daughter was illegally detained at the police station until 9 pm.

title: 'Why Defame Someone Who's Dead?' Twisha Sharma's Relative On In-Laws' Remarks
content: "It is ok to accuse. It is OK to defame the entire family... It is OK to do that for the living... but it is really shocking and appalling to do so for someone who's deceased," Rashi Sharma said in an exclusive interview to NDTV.

title: Gujarat Doctor Demands Rs 4,000 To Perform Free Surgery, Arrested
content: The accused has been charged under anti-corruption provisions for abusing his position and accepting illegal gratification.

title: Norway Reports Europe's 1st Case Of Bird Flu In A Polar Bear
content: Norwegian authorities on Tuesday announced that avian influenza has been documented in a polar bear for the first time in Europe, in the Svalbard region in the Arctic.

title: 'Nuclear Plants Must Not Be Targets': US Expert To NDTV After Strike In UAE
content: The Barakah incident comes at a time when nuclear infrastructure is increasingly finding itself near active conflict zones.

title: Rules Eased, US Eyes India's New $300-Billion Nuclear Goldmine
content: US nuclear companies happy with amendments in the Indian nuclear liability law, but want industry-friendly rules.

title: Trapped In Lift For 1.5 Hours, 7-Year-Old Boy Dies In Pune
content: Police said the lift began moving but stopped midway before reaching the second floor, leaving the child trapped inside.

title: CBI Charges Army Colonel, 5 Others In Rs 50 Lakh Bribery Case
content: The Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) has registered a regular case against a high-ranking Indian Army officer, a prominent defense contractor from Kanpur, and four others for their alleged involvement in a major corruption and bribery ring.

title: Nearly 100 Huts Demolished In Mumbai's Bandra, Drive To Continue Tomorrow
content: Chief Public Relations Officer Vineet Abhishek told PTI that the five-day drive aims to free 5,300 square metres of land to clear the railway safety zone and facilitate future capacity augmentation projects.

title: A Reservation Policy Collapsed. One Student Spent 9 Years Paying The Price
content: Around him are engineers, some junior to him in experience, some once his classmates in Maharashtra. But for nearly nine years, Sayyad says, he could not officially call himself one.

title: Days After Vijay Oath, CPM Threatens To Withdraw Support Over AIADMK Backing
content: Making any AIADMK faction part of the TVK government is against the people's mandate, which has been against the DMK and the AIADMK, the Left party said.

title: "Two-Three Days, Early Next Week": Trump's Timeline For Fresh Iran Attack
content: The comment follows Trump's recent statement on Sunday that "the clock is ticking" for Iran to accept a peace deal with the United States and "there won't be anything left of them" if Tehran's leaders don't "get moving, FAST".

title: Man Films Video Inside Chief Minister Vijay's Chamber, Sparks Row
content: During the meeting, one of the representatives recorded a video that panned to include the chief minister in the frame.

title: OpenAI Founding Member Andrej Karpathy Joins Anthropic
content: Former Tesla AI executive and one of OpenAI's founding members Andrej Karpathy has joined Anthropic, he said on Tuesday.

title: J&K Ticket Collector Turns Guardian For Lost Child, Reunites Her With Parents
content: Panic turned to relief at Jammu and Kashmir's Shri Mata Vaishno Devi Katra Railway Station when a lost girl was safely reunited with her parents on Tuesday.

title: Official Found Dead At Indian Mission In Bangladesh, Autopsy Awaited
content: The body was subsequently sent to the morgue at Chittagong Medical College Hospital for autopsy. The exact reason behind his death is not clear yet.

title: US Iran War News Live Updates: Trump Holds Off Iran Attack Planned For Tuesday After Gulf Intervention
content: US Iran War News LIVE Updates: Israel launched a massive series of airstrikes on southern Lebanon, despite an extension of the truce between the two countries.

title: 'Pushpa Has Bowed, Abhishek Banerjee Will Too': BJP Leader On Falta Repoll
content: Jahangir Khan withdrew from the contest earlier today, leaving Trinamool out of the race in a seat that it had earlier won and in a state where it had just lost power.

title: PM Modi Leaves For Italy After Norway Visit, India-Nordic Summit
content: During his two-day visit, PM Modi held separate talks with his counterparts, Norway Prime Minister Store, Iceland Prime Minister Kristrun Frostadottir, Finland Prime Minister Petteri Orpo and Denmark Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen.

title: Modi broke democratic tradition of asking, answering questions: Congress
content: Drawing parallels, he claimed that in the last 12 years, Leader of Opposition in Lok Sabha Rahul Gandhi has held 129 press conferences and answered countless unfiltered questions

title: Petrol, diesel prices up 90 paise; second hike within a week
content: The steepest hike was in Kolkata – in comparison to Delhi, Mumbai and Chennai – where the price of petrol was hiked by 96 paise to ₹109.70/litre and that of diesel by 94 paise to ₹96.07/litre.

title: Kozhikode native in Saudi jail to be released after legal formalities
content: A turning point in the case took place when the Malayali diaspora managed to raise ₹35 crore, the blood money the victim’s family had demanded, in May 2024

title: Commitment to democracy makes India, Nordic nations natural partners: Modi
content: India and Nordic countries announce ‘Green Tech and innovation Strategic Partnership’ with focus on sustainable energy and maritime research; joint statement condemns Pahalgam terror attack

title: Congress alleges mismanagement, corruption in ONGC and GSPC; seeks PM’s reply on fuel output decline
content: The party claims India has suffered an annual loss of 3.695 million metric tonnes in petrol, diesel, and gas production since 2014

title: Trouble mounts for Abhishek Banerjee as Kolkata civic body sends notice against his properties
content: In the last few days, Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari has been talking about properties owned by the Trinamool Congress general secretary and other Trinamool leaders that are under scrutiny

title: Trinamool Congress candidate for Falta Assembly seat Jahangir Khan withdraws from contest
content: Party condemns move, states decision to withdraw from the Falta re-poll is Jahangir Khan’s personal choice and not that of the party.

title: Sixth tiger dies of suspected CDV in Madhya Pradesh’s Kanha Reserve
content: While the previous five deaths took place in the Sarhi range, the latest death in the Mukki range raises concerns of a highly contagious virus spreading in the forest

title: Price mismatch may impede India’s battery storage projects: report
content: Tariffs discovered in 2025 auctions increasingly don’t appear to be economically viable, says the report

title: Twisha Sharma death: husband remains absconding as families trade charges
content: Sharma’s family demands second independent post-mortem due to suspicions of tampering with the first report; accuse Ms. Singh of “character assassination”, refute her claims regarding Sharma’s mental health

title: U.S. clears sale of $198 million support package for Apache attack helicopters
content: The possible sale of support services for the helicopters and the howitzers will take place under the Foreign Military Sale channels

title: Church leaders meet Naga group in attempt to mend fences with the Kuki-Zo
content: The secretary general of CBCNEI told journalists that the focus was on defusing the hostage crisis triggered by the killing of three Thadou church leaders on May 13

title: Retired teachers of Government Arts College at Thycaud gather to share memories
content: Government Arts College remains one of the finest educational institutions I have taught in, not just for its academics, but also for the human bonds it nurtured, says one of the organisers

title: Ensure foolproof and secure NEET, Education Minister says  at review meet
content: Coordination meetings to be held with District Magistrates and Superintendents of Police across the States to ‘ensure robust monitoring and effective implementation of examination arrangements’

title: Finance Minister hits back at Rahul Gandhi, saying the Congress should stop being obstructionist
content: Nirmala Sitharaman listed a number of achievements of the Modi government under several social sector schemes and asked whether these were for the benefit of ‘Adani/Ambani’

title: Supreme Court chides Education Secretary over logistical lapses in audit of private universities
content: The Bench had earlier issued a contempt notice to the Secretary, Department of Higher Education, on a matter related to its April 20 order directing the Centre to provide administrative support to a court-appointed panel

title: ASHAs to hold ‘victory march’ in Thiruvananthapuram on May 21
content: KAHWA members express satisfaction at the positive manner in which the new Kerala government took up their demand for decent wages.

title: NHRC asks Centre to review changes to trans law, ‘revisit’ community concerns on self-identification
content: NHRC says trans students should be admitted to educational institutions based on self-identified gender; calls for distinct Census columns to record “intersex” people, “transmen”, and “transwomen”

title: Supreme Court sends Dr Subbaiah murder case accused to life imprisonment
content: The trial court had sentenced seven accused, including the father and the two sons, to death for the murder in Agust 2021

title: OCI status of foreigners who  voted in Tamil Nadu Assembly election comes under scrutiny
content: The number of foreigners of Indian origin who voted in a fraudulent manner has increased to 30. Their declaration while applying for the OCI card that they had surrendered their voter identity cards or deleted their names from the electoral rolls will be treated as a false declaration and action will taken under law, sources say

title: Congress targets PM over fuel price hike, links his Norway trip to Adani group’s interests
content: Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge said giving a free pass to industrialist Gautam Adani and ‘robbing’ the common people is Narendra Modi’s ‘compromised model’; Lok Sabha LoP Rahul Gandhi wondered whether the PM’s personal request led to the Adani group being removed from a Norwegian wealth fund blacklist

title: Watch: MEA responds to Norwegian press over PM Modi not taking questions | Above the Fold | 19.05.2026
content: Above the Fold is The Hindu’s daily news show, bringing you the day’s biggest stories with clarity, depth, and context. In today’s episode, we look at the Supreme Court’s strong remarks on stray dogs, the MEA’s response to questions over PM Modi not taking media queries during his Norway visit, and more.

title: Caste slurs in Census forms: SC Commission issues notice to Punjab Census office
content: Notice also issued to Punjab Social Justice Department, action taken reports sought in 15 days; these caste slurs are also used in multiple State lists in the Constitution (Scheduled Castes) Order

title: Farmers oppose Bengaluru’s new international cricket stadium
content: Farmers say that the area for the proposed stadium lies in proximity to the Karadikkal-Mahadeshwara wildlife corridor, and that building a huge stadium in this area will have a serious and adverse impact on the environment and wildlife

title: PF details to be available on WhatsApp soon
content: Services, including viewing PF balance, last five transactions and claim status will be available to all subscribers within a month

title: Delhi Police may seek larger Bench reference on UAPA bail curbs amid ‘conflicting’ coordinate Bench rulings
content: Additional Solicitor General S.V. Raju said the apex court’s May 18 ruling overlooked that under the UAPA’s statutory bail bar, the presumption of innocence takes a ‘backseat’

title: Dalit families seek housing sites in Sriramarangapura village
content: Villagers also informed the authorities that discussions have been held to purchase land for formation of housing sites

title: Shia Muslims in Kashmir divided over moon sighting for Id-ul-Azha
content: The moon sighting grouping, Ruet-e-Hilal Committee, of the All J&K Shia Association (AJKSA) says the crescent moon of Zul Hijjah was not sighted on May 18 and hence Id will be celebrated on May 27, while Sunnis and some Shia faithfuls will observe it on May 28

title: Museums are repositories of knowledge, says historian
content: He was delivering a special lecture at a programme organised as part of International Museum Day at the Government Museum in Kalaburagi

title: National security linked to economic growth; strategic self-reliance a must: Gen. Dwivedi
content: General Upendra Dwivedi says dependence on foreign supply chains, critical minerals and digital infrastructure is a major strategic vulnerability for countries

title: A.P. Chief Minister advises Kavali MLA to improve performance of 13 departments
content: Chandrababu Naidu directs the officials of APSRTC, Endowments and Rural Water Supply departments to work diligently and meet the expectations of the people
